seoDescription: Partnership process and official contact pathway for research collaboration with the Centre for Plant Medicine Research.
lede: International partners, universities, and research agencies can assess CPMR capacity and follow a clear pathway for formal collaboration enquiries.
introParagraphs: Collaboration covers joint research, analytical services, capacity building, and access to specialist facilities where policies allow. Enquiries are reviewed against institutional priorities and regulatory obligations.
Use the process below before contacting the research partnerships desk. Operational contact details remain governed in this file for future CMS compatibility.

processSteps.title: Define scope and objectives
processSteps.description: Summarise the research question, expected outputs, timeline, and whether facility access or analytical services are required.

processSteps.title: Review facilities and departmental fit
processSteps.description: Consult the research facilities profiles and department mini-sites to confirm hosting units and technical feasibility.

processSteps.title: Submit a formal enquiry
processSteps.description: Contact the research partnerships desk with institutional affiliation, principal investigator details, and any ethics or export-control considerations.

processSteps.title: Institutional review
processSteps.description: CPMR reviews the proposal against mandate, capacity, and partnership policy. Approved collaborations proceed to memoranda or service agreements as required.

contactIntro: For research collaboration and partnership enquiries, use the institutional email below. Do not send patient-specific or clinical triage requests to this pathway.

contact.label: Research partnerships desk
contact.responseNote: Allow up to ten working days for an initial response. Urgent operational matters should use official clinic or service contact routes when published.
